posts.title: How Can Poor Oral Condition be Injurious to Overall Health?
posts.excerpt: F. Lee McLemore, DDS blog offers insights and tips on maintaining optimal oral hygiene to promote better health and combat the negative effects of poor oral health.
posts.contentHtml:
Your dental health is a part of your overall physical well-being, keeping the oral issues unaddressed can pose major threats in no time. Just like you get your body checked after getting exposed to fever, do you consider visiting a dentist while dealing with bleeding gums?

A healthy lifestyle is ensured when every body part functions normally- even a little concern must be highlighted, treated and solved! In case you are suffering from poor oral conditions, book an appointment in a dental emergency care in Granbury, TX, as soon as possible. What happens if you don’t prioritize your oral health? Check out the possible consequences stated below.

Cardiovascular Disease
  • Heart and gum diseases are interconnected. Infected gums make it easier for the bacteria to make their way into the heart. As a result, the origin of cardiovascular diseases takes place.
  • It can expose you to strokes.
  • Hypertension or high blood pressure.
  • Endocarditis- this is a condition where the inner lining of your heart’s valves and chambers gets infected.
Leading Cause Responsible for Diabetes
  • When you have poor oral health, it can eventually cause difficulty in controlling your blood sugar level.
  • It boosts the risk of suffering from diabetes.
  • Individuals with diabetes might face kidney problems after a certain point in time.
  • Diabetes is a prominent reason responsible for delayed wound healing and vision problems.
  • The blend of both oral concerns and diabetes can lead to nerve damage. Mitigate such problems by booking timely appointments with us.
Respiratory Problems
  • Your poor state of oral health increases pneumonia risk.
  • It leads to Halitosis or in simple words, bad breath.
  • Your oral health is also held accountable for COPD or Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease.
  • Inhaling harmful bacteria results in a lot of respiratory tract infections.
Cognitive Decline and Dementia
  • Inflammation and chronic oral infections might expose you to systemic inflammation.
  • It causes reduced nutrition and chronic stress factors.
  • Porphyromonas gingivalis is a bacteria that causes gum disease and is present in an Alzheimer patient’s brain as well.
Complications in Pregnancy
  • Low birth weight or premature birth.
  • Gestational diabetes.
  • Respiratory problems such as asthma.
  • Preeclampsia is a condition that takes place because of inflammation and gum infections. It can be characterized by liver and kidney damage as well as high blood pressure.
  • Bacteria transmission is a serious threat that can be harmful to newborn babies and children. You can get in touch with a children’s dental specialist in Cleburne, TX.
Kidney Disease
  • Inflammation and infections in your kidney can occur due to poor oral health.
  • High blood pressure.
  • It can lead to bacterial spread.
  • Certain medications that are used to treat your oral situation can ultimately cause harm to your kidney.
Issues in Proper Digestion
  • Reduced production of enzymes.
  • Face difficulties in chewing.
  • An imbalance in your gum microbiome.
  • Esophageal issues.
Rheumatoid Arthritis
  • It can hamper your immune system and increase your chronic pain.
  • It leads to chronic inflammation.
Keep Your Dental Health in Check- Look out for These Symptoms

Oral problems can be detected through some symptoms, we’ve outlined them right below:

  • Swollen or bleeding gums after flossing and brushing
  • Sores and ulcers inside your mouth that take a prolonged time to heal
  • Extreme sensitivity to beverages with cold and hot temperatures
  • Chronic bad breath
  • Loose teeth
  • Toothache or pain
  • Suffering from pain while biting or chewing
  • Receding gums
  • Swelling of your cheek and gums
  • Broken or cracked teeth
  • Jaw clicking
  • Experiencing dry mouth

Do you know frequent dental checkups won’t leave any room for these symptoms as well? Book an appointment with Weatherford family dentistry at least twice a year and you will be good to go.

What Are the Causes Responsible for Oral and Dental Diseases?

Bad oral health can take place due to a lot of possible causes, some of which typically include:

  • Snacking on drinks and food on a regular basis
  • Smoking
  • Diabetes
  • Inadequate brushing habits
  • Genetics or family history
  • Medications that reduce the presence of saliva inside your mouth
  • Hormonal changes in women
  • Heartburn or acid reflux
Here’s a Quick Brief on Oral Health Statistics

Centers for Disease Control and Prevention clearly states these few statistics:

  • 52% of the children suffer from cavities in their baby teeth by age 8.
  • Most of the adults belonging to the age group 20 to 64 have cavities in their mouths.
  • Emergency and unplanned dental care leads to a loss of 34 million school hours in the US.
  • Untreated dental conditions result in a loss of $45 billion in the US.
